market_briefing.summary: Global markets on March 20, 2026, are navigating a complex environment shaped by Middle East geopolitical tensions, a hawkish shift across major central banks, and rotation concerns following recent tech stock volatility. WTI crude eased to ~$93/barrel, reflecting potential de-escalation rhetoric, though energy markets remain sensitive. Central banks (Fed, RBA, ECB) are maintaining restrictive stances to combat persistent inflation.
highlights: Geopolitical tensions (Middle East) continue to dominate market sentiment, driving commodity volatility.
Crude oil prices (WTI) eased to ~$93.43/barrel on signs of potential de-escalation.
Global central banks remain hawkish; Fed projects minimal rate cuts for 2026 due to sticky inflation.
Mixed equity performance: US markets rotating out of 'Magnificent 7' tech stocks amid AI disruption fears; Indian markets saw a rebound.
Safe-haven assets (Gold/USD) remain supported despite recent daily volatility.
